# Verify that re-adding an existing secret with different groups records a
# secret.change_access entry (not a second add) and updates its access list.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# First add: admin-only.
exec sesam add some_file.txt
exists .sesam/objects/some_file.txt.sesam
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"secret.add"'

# Re-add the same path with a group => this is a change_access, not a new add.
exec sesam add some_file.txt --group dev
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"secret.change_access"'
exec sesam log
stdout 'changed access of some_file.txt to dev'
exec sesam verify

# The secret list reflects the new group (admin stays implicit).
exec sesam ls
stdout 'dev'

-- some_file.txt --
Some content
